title: Metric–Topological Collapse Duality
core_statement: Topological viability may fail before metric viability fully vanishes.

metric_collapse.definition: Metric collapse occurs when recoverable transformational accessibility measure approaches exhaustion.

topological_collapse.definition: Topological collapse occurs when recoverable accessibility connectivity degenerates.

precollapse_condition.statement: Accessible futures may still exist while no longer forming a sufficiently connected recoverable structure.

interpretive_consequence: A system may appear metrically viable while already losing topological viability through fragmentation, bottlenecks, or loss of return pathways.
one_sentence_lock: Accessible futures still exist, but they no longer form a sufficiently connected recoverable structure.
